---
title: "STA137 Final Project"
author: "Caspar Leung and Elizabeth Louie"
date: "2026-03-05"
output: html_document
---


```{r}
library(forecast)
library(ggplot2)

# Data Acquisition 

# import data file
df <- read.csv("multiTimeline.csv", skip = 2, header = TRUE)

# rename columns
colnames(df) <- c("Time", "Interest")

df$Time <- substr(df$Time, 1, 19)

# check the first few rows
head(df)
summary(df$Interest)
```

```{r}
# Data Visualization 

# scatter plot of original series 
ggplot(df, aes(x = Time, y = Interest)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  labs(
    title = "Scatter Plot: Search Interest Over Time",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Interest"
  )

# histogram showing the distribution of Interest rating
hist(df$Interest,
     main = "Histogram of BTS Search Interest",
     xlab = "Interest")

```

```{r}
# Data Preparation 

# identify outliers
df[which.max(df$Interest), ]

ggplot(df, aes(x = Time, y = Interest)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  geom_point(
    data = df[df$Time == "2026-01-01", ],
    color = "red",
    size = 3
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  labs(
    title = "Scatter Plot: Search Interest Over Time",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Interest Score"
  )

# removal of outliers 
df_clean <- df[df$Time != "2026-01-01", ]

# scatter plot after outlier removal 
ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = Time, y = Interest)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Scatter Plot: Search Interest Over Time (Outlier Removed)",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Interest Score"
  )

# check for missing values 
sum(is.na(df$Interest)) # output = 0 

```


```{r}
# Data Preparation 

# convert data to time series object   
bts_ts <- ts(df_clean$Interest, frequency = 12, start = c(2021,3)) 

# boxcox lambda approximation for varaince stability 
BoxCox.lambda(bts_ts) # output -0.4964918

# apply boxcox transformation 
bts_bc <- BoxCox(bts_ts, lambda = -0.4964918)

# plot of transformed series 
plot(bts_bc,
     main = "Histogram of Box-Cox Transformed BTS Interest",
     ylab = "Transformed Interest",
     xlab = "Time")

# histogram 
hist(bts_bc,
     main = "Histogram of Log-Transformed BTS Interest",
     xlab = "log(Interest)")

# QQ plot
qqnorm(bts_bc)
qqline(bts_bc)
```


```{r}
# Analyzing the "Smooth" Component: Trend and Seasonality Elimination 

# early period 
bts_early <- window(bts_bc, end = c(2022, 6))

# later period 
bts_late <- window(bts_bc, start = c(2022, 6))

# apply moving average filter to first part 
hatm_early <- filter(bts_early, sides = 2, c(0.5, rep(1,11), 0.5)/12)

# apply moving average filter to second part
hatm_late <- filter(bts_late, sides = 2, c(0.5, rep(1,11), 0.5)/12)

# time series object of original data with outlier 
bts_ts_full <- ts(df$Interest, frequency = 12, start = c(2021,3))
bts_bc_full <- BoxCox(bts_ts_full, lambda = -0.4964918)

# apply moving average filter to data with outlier 
hatm_full <- filter(bts_bc_full, sides = 2, c(0.5, rep(1,11), 0.5)/12)

# estimated trends with outlier 
plot(bts_bc,
     main="Trend Estimate Not including outl",
     ylab="Interest",
     xlab="Time")
lines(hatm_early, col="blue", lwd=2)
lines(hatm_late, col="red", lwd=2)

# estimated trend plot without outlier 
plot(bts_bc_full,
     main="Trend Estimate Including Outlier",
     ylab="Interest",
     xlab="Time")

lines(hatm_full, col="purple", lwd=2)

# remove trend component 
#detrended_ts <- bts_bc - hatm

# HW2 seasonal matrix method 
A <- matrix(detrended_ts, ncol = 12, byrow = TRUE)

# estimated seasonal effect for each month
mu <- array(0, 12)

for (k in 1:6) {
  mu[k] <- mean(A[2:5, k], na.rm = TRUE)
}
for (k in 7:12) {
  mu[k] <- mean(A[1:4, k], na.rm = TRUE)
}

# seasonal effects sum to zero 
hats <- rep(mu - mean(mu), 5)

# make seasonal component into time series object 
seasonal_component <- ts(hats, start = c(2021, 3), frequency = 12)

plot(seasonal_component,
     main = "Estimated Seasonal Component",
     ylab = "Seasonality",
     xlab = "Time")

# residuals after eliminating trend and seasonality
smooth_resid <- bts_bc - hatm - seasonal_component

plot(smooth_resid,
     main = "Residual Series After Removing Trend and Seasonality",
     ylab = "Residuals",
     xlab = "Time")
abline(h = 0, col = "red")

# ACF and PCAF residual analysis check 
acf(na.omit(as.numeric(smooth_resid)),
    main = "ACF of Residuals")
pacf(na.omit(as.numeric(smooth_resid)),
     main = "PACF of Residuals")
```







```{r}
#Analyzing the “rough” component: model fitting & diagnostics


# Residual analysis 
acf(na.omit(as.numeric(smooth_resid)),
    main = "ACF of Residuals")

pacf(na.omit(as.numeric(smooth_resid)),
     main = "PACF of Residuals")

#ordered

# Fit candidate models 
fit_ar1 <- Arima(smooth_resid, order = c(1,0,0))
fit_ma1 <- Arima(smooth_resid, order = c(0,0,1))
fit_arma11 <- Arima(smooth_resid, order = c(1,0,1))

# Compare by AIC
AIC(fit_ar1, fit_ma1, fit_arma11)

# Choose best AR(1)
best_fit <- fit_ar1 # model with lowest AIC 

# Diagnostics on innovations
innov <- residuals(best_fit)

acf(na.omit(innov), main = "ACF of ARMA Innovations")
pacf(na.omit(innov), main = "PACF of ARMA Innovations")

#conclusion either AR(1) or ARMA(1,1)
```

```{r}
#Predict future values:

#Forecast Parameters
h <- 12  
n <- length(bts_ts)
future_t <- (n + 1):(n + h)

#Predict the Smooth Component: Trend
# quadratic model
trend_forecast <- predict(trend_fit, newdata = data.frame(t = future_t))

#Predict the Smooth Component: Seasonality
seasonal_cycle <- seasonal_component[1:12]
seasonal_forecast <- rep(seasonal_cycle, length.out = h)

#Predict the Rough Component: AR(1)
final_rough_model <- arima(smooth_resid, order = c(1, 0, 0))

#predict function Section 3.5: Linear Prediction Filters
rough_pred_obj <- predict(final_rough_model, n.ahead = h)
rough_forecast <- rough_pred_obj$pred
rough_se <- rough_pred_obj$se  # Standard error for prediction intervals

#Combine Components for Final Prediction

final_prediction <- trend_forecast + seasonal_forecast + rough_forecast

#95% Prediction Intervals
lower_pi <- final_prediction - 1.96 * rough_se
upper_pi <- final_prediction + 1.96 * rough_se


#Visualization of Results

# Convert forecasts to time series objects starting Mar 2026
forecast_ts <- ts(final_prediction, start = c(2026, 3), frequency = 12)
lower_ts <- ts(lower_pi, start = c(2026, 3), frequency = 12)
upper_ts <- ts(upper_pi, start = c(2026, 3), frequency = 12)

# Create the plot
plot(bts_ts, 
     xlim = c(2021, 2027.5), 
     ylim = c(min(lower_pi), max(bts_ts) + 10),
     main = "BTS Search Interest: 12-Month Forecast",
     ylab = "Interest Score", 
     xlab = "Year",
     lwd = 2)

# Add the point forecast line
lines(forecast_ts, col = "blue", lwd = 2)

# Add the 95% Prediction Interval (dashed lines)
lines(lower_ts, col = "red", lty = 2)
lines(upper_ts, col = "red", lty = 2)

# Add a legend
legend("topright", 
       legend = c("Observed Data", "AR(1) Forecast", "95% Pred. Interval"),
       col = c("black", "blue", "red"), 
       lty = c(1, 1, 2), 
       lwd = c(2, 2, 1),
       cex = 0.8)

print(data.frame(
  Month = seq(as.Date("2026-03-01"), by="month", length.out=h),
  Forecast = as.numeric(final_prediction),
  Lower_95 = as.numeric(lower_pi),
  Upper_95 = as.numeric(upper_pi)
))
```
